zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert Sinclair於1190年出生時，他的父親Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clair31歲，母親Isabella de Rosslin26歲。他於1213年與Eleanor de Dreux在法國洛易-吉黑豪蒙吉素-洛易結婚。他們至少是2個兒子的父母。他去世於1248年4月之後，埋葬在法國。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Robert Sinclair于1190年出生时，他的父亲Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clair31岁，母亲Isabella de Rosslin26岁。他于1213年与Eleanor de Dreux在法国洛易-吉黑豪蒙吉素-洛易结婚。他们至少是2个儿子的父母。他去世于1248年4月之后，埋葬在法国。

nameMeanings.name: Sinclair
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish and English: of Norman origin, a habitational name from Saint-Clair-sur-Elle (Manche), so called from the dedication of its church to Saint Clarus (see   Clare   3).
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

History: The Scottish family Sinclair (see 1 above) came to southern Scotland in the twelfth century from Huntingdonshire with the de Morvilles. Descendants of these Sinclairs became earls of Caithness in the 15th century. The frequency of the surname in Caithness and Orkney is largely due to the adoption by tenants of the name of their overlord.

variants.name: Clare
meanings: Irish (Clare, Wicklow, and Wexford): habitational name from Clare in Suffolk (an eastern county of England), the center of a major Norman baronial estate since the Norman Conquest. The English placename probably originated as a Celtic river name meaning ‘bright, gentle, or warm’. The surname is not from the Irish county name Clare, which is either from the surname or from a place called with Gaelic clár ‘plank bridge’. Another Norman surname in the southeast of Ireland was de Clere, from Clères in Seine-Maritime (France), named from the local river.

English and French: from the Middle English, Old French female personal name Cla(i)re (from Latin Clara, from clarus ‘clear, bright, famous’) which achieved some popularity, greater elsewhere in Europe than in England, through the fame of Saint Clare of Assisi (see Chiara ). The English surname seems to have been interchangeable with Clear 1.

English: occupational name from an agent derivative of Middle English cley ‘clay’. The work of medieval clayers involved plastering a framework of interwoven twigs with mud to produce wattle-and-daub work.

variants.name: St. Clair
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Sinclair  . This is also a French Canadian and American French altered form of the same surname.
French (Saint-Clair): habitational name from any of several places, e.g. in Normandy, named Saint-Clair (from the dedication of their churches to Saint Clarus). Compare Saintclair .

Haitian (also Saint-Clair): ornamental name or nickname, perhaps ironic, composed of French saint ‘saint’ or ‘holy’ and clair ‘clear’ or ‘lightness’. Alternatively, perhaps of the same French origin as 2 above. Compare Saintclair .

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

en.lifeSummary: When Robert Sinclair was born in 1190, his father, Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clair, was 31 and his mother, Isabella de Rosslin, was 26. He married Eleanor de Dreux in 1213, in Chaumont-sur-Loire, Loir-et-Cher, France. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died after April 1248, and was buried in France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert Sinclair est né en 1190, son père, Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clair, avait 31 ans et sa mère, Isabella de Rosslin, avait 26 ans. Il a épousé Eleanor de Dreux en 1213, à Chaumont-sur-Loire, Loir-et-Cher, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé après avril 1248, et a été enterré à France.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert Sinclair 1190 geboren wurde, war sein Vater Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clair 31 und seine Mutter Isabella de Rosslin 26. Er hat 1213 in Chaumont-sur-Loire, Loir-et-Cher, Frankreich Eleanor de Dreux geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Söhne. Er starb nach April 1248 und wurde in Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Sinclair è nato il nel 1190, il suo padre, Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clair, aveva 31 anni e il suo madre, Isabella de Rosslin, aveva 26 anni. Lui sposato Eleanor de Dreux nel 1213, nel Chaumont-sur-Loire, Loir-et-Cher, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figli. Morì dopo il aprile 1248, e fu sepolto a Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert Sinclairが1190年に生まれた時，父Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clairは31歳，母Isabella de Rosslinは26歳であった。1213年にフランスロイル-エト-チャーチャウモント-スル-ロイアーでEleanor de Dreuxと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の息子の親であった。彼は1248年4月以後に死去し，フランスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert Sinclair(이)가 1190년에 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clair(은)는 31세였고, 그의 어머니 Isabella de Rosslin(은)는 26세였다. 그는 1213년 프랑스 로이-트-처 차몽-수-로이르에서 Eleanor de Dreux(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1248년 4월 이후 사망했으며 프랑스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert Sinclair nasceu em 1190, seu pai, Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clair, tinha 31 anos e sua mãe, Isabella de Rosslin, tinha 26 anos. Ele casou-se com Eleanor de Dreux em 1213, em Chaumont-sur-Loire, Loir-et-Cher, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhos. Ele faleceu após abril de 1248, e foi sepultado em França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert Sinclair родился в 1190 г., его отцу по имени Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clair было 31 год, а матери, которую звали Isabella de Rosslin, 26. Он женился на женщине по имени Eleanor de Dreux в 1213 г. в местности чаумонт-сур-лоир, лоир-ет-чир, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына. Он умер после апрель 1248 г. и был похоронен в местности Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert Sinclair nació en 1190, su papá, Amaury Robert de Chaumont Lord St Clair, tenía 31 años y su mamá, Isabella de Rosslin, tenía 26 años. Se casó con Eleanor de Dreux en 1213, en Chaumont-sur-Loire, Loir-et-Cher, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijos. Murió después de abril de 1248, y fue sepultado en Francia.
